full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic additively manufactured (AM) weight-reduction bracket. Replace an existing machined mounting bracket with a topology-optimized AM part that preserves the same interface and load-carrying capability while reducing mass by at least 50%. Bracket connects a 10 kg payload to a rectangular mounting face via 4x M6 bolts on a 80 mm x 60 mm rectangular pattern. Bounding envelope: 120 mm x 100 mm x 60 mm. Payload attaches at a single M10 threaded boss located 90 mm from the mounting face. Material: SLS/DMLS AlSi10Mg (aluminum 3D print), E = 70 GPa, yield = 220 MPa, ultimate = 440 MPa, density = 2670 kg/m^3. Baseline machined bracket mass 350 g (solid block equivalent); target AM part mass <= 175 g (50% reduction). Load cases: (R1) Static payload 10 kg x 9.81 = 98 N at the M10 boss, vertically downward; max von Mises <= yield / 2.0 = 110 MPa. (R2) Transport shock 5 g x 10 kg = 490 N in the worst-case direction (45 deg from vertical in the YZ plane); max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 147 MPa. (R3) Side-load maneuver 2 g lateral = 196 N at the boss; max von Mises <= 110 MPa. (R4) Mass <= 175 g AND mass reduction vs baseline >= 50%. (R5) Displacement at the M10 boss under LC1 <= 0.5 mm. (R6) Modal first frequency hard-mounted at four M6 holes >= 120 Hz (to avoid coupling with typical chassis vibration at 60 Hz 2x margin). Primary verification: structural FEA for R1-R3 + R5, modal for R6. Vibration secondary via R6. Thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling explicitly NOT required. AM-specific constraint: all overhangs >= 45 deg from build plate OR include sacrificial support, and no internal voids without drain holes of at least 3 mm diameter. Topology optimization required as the outer design loop. Deliverables: STEP AP242, build orientation recommendation, and a report listing mass in g, mass reduction %, max von Mises per case in MPa, displacement at boss in mm, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic additively manufactured AlSi10Mg mounting bracket that replaces an existing machined bracket supporting a 10 kg payload, preserving the same interface (4x M6 bolts on an 80 x 60 mm pattern on the mount face, single M10 threaded payload boss 90 mm from the mount face) while reducing mass by at least 50% against the 350 g machined baseline.

Envelope: bounding box 120 x 100 x 60 mm. DfAM rules apply (overhangs >= 45 deg or sacrificial support; no internal voids without >= 3 mm drain holes). Topology optimization is required as the outer loop.

Load cases.
- LC1 static payload: 10 kg x 9.81 = 98 N at the M10 boss, vertical, boss displacement <= 0.5 mm.
- LC2 transport shock: 5 g x 10 kg = 490 N at the boss, worst-case 45 deg direction in the YZ plane.
- LC3 side-load maneuver: 2 g lateral = 196 N at the boss.
- LC4 modal: hard-mounted at the four M6 holes, first frequency clear of ~60 Hz chassis vibration by 2 x margin.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 50% mass reduction vs baseline is the scoring driver; topo-opt calls structural FEA inside.

prompt.title: Additively manufactured weight-reduction bracket
prompt.brief: AlSi10Mg AM bracket replacing a 350 g machined baseline; must be <= 175 g, survive 3 static load cases, meet displacement and modal limits.

boundary_conditions.mounting: four M6 bolt holes fully fixed

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: AM residual stress and warpage are manufacturing qualification steps, out of scope here.

eval_coverage: AM process constraints (overhang angle, drain holes)
